content:
# Plan and Implement

## Change Summary

Added INVARIANT documentation comments at both json_fill_none call sites in StartJournalDetails::merge (fs.rs:2326) and CompletionJournalDetails::merge (fs.rs:2371). The comments document that json_fill_none keeps the base value when both sides are present, and that any new optional field where conflicting values should be rejected MUST have an explicit option_conflicts check added before the json_fill_none call — otherwise conflicting values are silently preserved from the existing record rather than flagged as an error. This makes the contract between conflict-checking and null-filling explicit for future maintainers.

## Steps

1. [Completed] Added INVARIANT comment block at StartJournalDetails::merge json_fill_none call site (fs.rs:2326): documents that json_fill_none keeps the base value when both sides are present, and that any new optional field with conflict semantics MUST have an explicit option_conflicts check added before the call
2. [Completed] Added matching INVARIANT reference comment at CompletionJournalDetails::merge json_fill_none call site (fs.rs:2371): cross-references StartJournalDetails::merge and restates the requirement for explicit conflict checks on new fields
3. [Completed] Ran cargo test (1165 total: 353 lib + 128 integration + 1 doc + 683 unit, 0 failed), cargo clippy -- -D warnings (clean), cargo fmt --check on src/adapters/fs.rs (clean)

## Validation Evidence

- cargo test: 1165 total tests passed (353 lib + 128 integration + 1 doc + 683 unit), 0 failed
- cargo clippy -- -D warnings: no warnings or errors
- cargo fmt --check: src/adapters/fs.rs clean; only pre-existing formatting diffs in unrelated tests/unit/agent_execution_test.rs
- StartJournalDetails::merge comment documents: json_fill_none keeps base value when both sides present; new optional fields with conflict semantics MUST add option_conflicts check before json_fill_none call
- CompletionJournalDetails::merge comment cross-references StartJournalDetails::merge INVARIANT and restates the requirement
- Comment-only change — no behavioral changes, all prior round tests continue to pass unchanged

## Outstanding Risks

- Pre-existing formatting drift in tests/unit/agent_execution_test.rs is unrelated to these changes but will appear in cargo fmt --check across the full workspace.
